datasets.name: rum_page_loads
datasets.description: Real User Monitoring page load events with navigation timing, Core Web Vitals, and distributed tracing context

datasets.name: rum_user_interactions
datasets.description: User interaction events including clicks, form submissions, and custom banking workflow actions

datasets.name: rum_resource_timing
datasets.description: Resource loading timing for scripts, stylesheets, images, and API calls

datasets.name: backend_services
datasets.description: Backend APM service spans linked to frontend traces via trace_id for distributed tracing correlation

datasets.name: application_catalog
datasets.description: Application metadata with business context, performance targets, and workflow definitions

datasets.name: user_sessions
datasets.description: Aggregated session-level metadata for user journey analysis and session replay context

queries.name: Frontend-to-Backend Performance Correlation via Distributed Tracing
queries.pain_point: Inability to connect frontend navigation with backend requests through distributed tracing
queries.query:
FROM rum_page_loads
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 24 hours AND total_page_load_ms > 3000
| LOOKUP JOIN backend_services ON trace_id
| LOOKUP JOIN application_catalog ON application_id
| EVAL frontend_time_ms = total_page_load_ms - backend_services.duration_ms
| EVAL backend_contribution_pct = (backend_services.duration_ms / total_page_load_ms) * 100
| WHERE backend_contribution_pct > 30
| STATS 
    avg_total_load = AVG(total_page_load_ms),
    avg_backend_time = AVG(backend_services.duration_ms),
    avg_frontend_time = AVG(frontend_time_ms),
    avg_db_time = AVG(backend_services.database_query_time_ms),
    slow_page_count = COUNT(*)
  BY application_catalog.application_name, backend_services.service_name
| SORT avg_total_load DESC
| LIMIT 20
queries.description: Correlates slow page loads with backend service performance using trace_id to identify whether bottlenecks originate from frontend rendering, backend processing, or database queries. Shows percentage contribution of each layer.

queries.name: Navigation Timing Phase Breakdown for Performance Diagnosis
queries.pain_point: Limited insight into performance bottlenecks across different phases of page load (DNS, TCP, request/response, DOM processing)
queries.query:
FROM rum_page_loads
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 7 days
| LOOKUP JOIN application_catalog ON application_id
| EVAL network_time = dns_time_ms + tcp_time_ms + ttfb_ms + response_time_ms
| EVAL processing_time = dom_processing_ms + load_event_ms
| EVAL network_pct = (network_time / total_page_load_ms) * 100
| EVAL processing_pct = (processing_time / total_page_load_ms) * 100
| INLINESTATS 
    avg_dns = AVG(dns_time_ms),
    avg_tcp = AVG(tcp_time_ms),
    avg_ttfb = AVG(ttfb_ms),
    avg_response = AVG(response_time_ms),
    avg_dom = AVG(dom_processing_ms)
  BY application_catalog.application_name, device_type
| EVAL dns_vs_avg = dns_time_ms - avg_dns
| EVAL tcp_vs_avg = tcp_time_ms - avg_tcp
| EVAL ttfb_vs_avg = ttfb_ms - avg_ttfb
| EVAL dom_vs_avg = dom_processing_ms - avg_dom
| WHERE total_page_load_ms > application_catalog.target_lcp_ms
| KEEP application_catalog.application_name, page_name, device_type, connection_type,
       dns_time_ms, dns_vs_avg, tcp_time_ms, tcp_vs_avg, ttfb_ms, ttfb_vs_avg,
       response_time_ms, dom_processing_ms, dom_vs_avg, network_pct, processing_pct
| SORT dns_vs_avg DESC, tcp_vs_avg DESC, ttfb_vs_avg DESC
| LIMIT 100
queries.description: Breaks down page load into distinct timing phases (DNS, TCP, TTFB, response download, DOM processing) and compares each phase against application-specific averages to identify which phase is causing performance degradation. Highlights outliers by device and connection type.

queries.name: Individual User Session Timeline with Interaction & Error Correlation
queries.pain_point: Lack of visibility into specific user interactions beyond page navigation (button clicks, form submissions, custom user actions)
queries.query:
FROM rum_page_loads
| WHERE session_id == ?session_id
| LOOKUP JOIN user_sessions ON session_id
| LOOKUP JOIN application_catalog ON application_id
| EVAL event_type = "page_load"
| EVAL event_detail = CONCAT(page_name, " (LCP: ", TO_STRING(lcp_ms), "ms, Errors: ", TO_STRING(error_count), ")")
| KEEP @timestamp, event_type, event_detail, application_catalog.application_name, user_sessions.workflow_name, user_sessions.workflow_completed
| SORT @timestamp ASC
queries.description: Provides a chronological timeline of all page loads for a specific user session, enriched with application context and workflow progress. Designed to be used with a parameterized session_id. Can be combined with rum_user_interactions in a FUSE query for complete session replay context.

queries.name: Core Web Vitals Monitoring by Application Type & Business Context
queries.pain_point: Difficulty in defining 'satisfactory performance' - each application and business has unique performance definitions, challenging to establish universal benchmarks based on Core Web Vitals
queries.query:
FROM rum_page_loads
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30 days
| LOOKUP JOIN application_catalog ON application_id
| EVAL lcp_meets_target = CASE(lcp_ms <= application_catalog.target_lcp_ms, 1, 0)
| EVAL fid_meets_target = CASE(fid_ms <= application_catalog.target_fid_ms, 1, 0)
| EVAL inp_meets_target = CASE(inp_ms <= application_catalog.target_inp_ms, 1, 0)
| EVAL cls_meets_target = CASE(cls_score <= application_catalog.target_cls, 1, 0)
| EVAL all_targets_met = CASE(lcp_meets_target == 1 AND fid_meets_target == 1 AND inp_meets_target == 1 AND cls_meets_target == 1, 1, 0)
| STATS
    p75_lcp = PERCENTILE(lcp_ms, 75),
    p75_fid = PERCENTILE(fid_ms, 75),
    p75_inp = PERCENTILE(inp_ms, 75),
    p75_cls = PERCENTILE(cls_score, 75),
    lcp_compliance_pct = AVG(lcp_meets_target) * 100,
    fid_compliance_pct = AVG(fid_meets_target) * 100,
    inp_compliance_pct = AVG(inp_meets_target) * 100,
    cls_compliance_pct = AVG(cls_meets_target) * 100,
    overall_compliance_pct = AVG(all_targets_met) * 100,
    page_load_count = COUNT(*)
  BY application_catalog.application_name, application_catalog.application_type, application_catalog.business_unit, application_catalog.criticality, device_type
| SORT overall_compliance_pct ASC, page_load_count DESC
| LIMIT 50
queries.description: Measures Core Web Vitals compliance against application-specific performance targets (not generic thresholds). Shows P75 metrics and compliance percentage by application type, business unit, and device. Identifies which critical applications are failing to meet their unique SLA targets.

queries.name: Resource Loading Optimization with CDN Performance Analysis
queries.pain_point: Resource Loading Optimization - analyze resource timing to optimize asset delivery and CDN performance
queries.query:
FROM rum_resource_timing
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 7 days AND http_status >= 200 AND http_status < 400
| LOOKUP JOIN application_catalog ON application_id
| EVAL cache_miss = CASE(is_cached == false, 1, 0)
| EVAL slow_resource = CASE(duration_ms > 1000, 1, 0)
| EVAL size_mb = transfer_size_bytes / 1048576
| STATS
    total_resources = COUNT(*),
    avg_duration_ms = AVG(duration_ms),
    p95_duration_ms = PERCENTILE(duration_ms, 95),
    avg_ttfb_ms = AVG(ttfb_ms),
    avg_download_ms = AVG(download_time_ms),
    total_transfer_mb = SUM(size_mb),
    cache_miss_rate_pct = AVG(cache_miss) * 100,
    slow_resource_pct = AVG(slow_resource) * 100
  BY application_catalog.application_name, resource_type, cdn_provider
| WHERE total_resources > 100
| SORT p95_duration_ms DESC, cache_miss_rate_pct DESC
| LIMIT 30
queries.description: Analyzes resource loading performance by type (scripts, stylesheets, images, fonts) and CDN provider. Identifies optimization opportunities through high cache miss rates, slow P95 load times, and excessive transfer sizes. Helps prioritize CDN configuration and asset optimization efforts.

queries.name: Conversion Funnel Drop-off Analysis with Workflow Tracking
queries.pain_point: Building Aggregated Conversion Funnels - identify drop-off points in account opening, loan application, card activation flows
queries.query:
FROM rum_user_interactions
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30 days AND workflow_name == ?workflow_name
| LOOKUP JOIN user_sessions ON session_id
| LOOKUP JOIN application_catalog ON application_id
| EVAL step_completed = CASE(resulted_in_error == false, 1, 0)
| STATS
    sessions_reached = COUNT(DISTINCT session_id),
    interactions = COUNT(*),
    success_rate_pct = AVG(step_completed) * 100,
    avg_interactions_per_session = COUNT(*) / COUNT(DISTINCT session_id),
    error_rate_pct = AVG(CASE(resulted_in_error == true, 1, 0)) * 100
  BY workflow_step, user_sessions.customer_segment, user_sessions.device_type
| SORT workflow_step ASC
| LIMIT 100
queries.description: Tracks user progression through banking workflows (PIX transfer, loan application, card activation) step-by-step. Identifies drop-off points by calculating sessions that reach each step, success rates, and error rates. Segments by customer type and device to uncover friction points in critical conversion funnels.

queries.name: Semantic Search for Similar Performance Issues Across Applications
queries.pain_point: Difficulty in identifying patterns between long-running tasks and user-reported issues across different applications
queries.query:
FROM application_catalog
| WHERE SEMANTIC(application_description, "customer-facing transactional applications with heavy JavaScript execution and complex DOM manipulation")
| LOOKUP JOIN rum_page_loads ON application_id
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 14 days AND long_task_count > 3
| EVAL severe_blocking = CASE(tbt_ms > 600, 1, 0)
| STATS
    avg_long_tasks = AVG(long_task_count),
    avg_tbt_ms = AVG(tbt_ms),
    avg_inp_ms = AVG(inp_ms),
    severe_blocking_pct = AVG(severe_blocking) * 100,
    avg_error_count = AVG(error_count),
    affected_pages = COUNT(DISTINCT page_name)
  BY application_catalog.application_name, application_catalog.application_description
| SORT avg_tbt_ms DESC
| LIMIT 15
queries.description: Uses semantic search to find applications with similar characteristics (e.g., 'heavy JavaScript', 'complex forms', 'real-time updates'), then analyzes their long task patterns and Total Blocking Time. Helps identify common performance anti-patterns across applications with similar architecture or user experience requirements.
